records.description: University-endorsed text-based generative AI access via University login and protected mode.
records.howToObtain: Students access copilot.cloud.microsoft and log in with UniKey using Okta.

evidence.sourceTitle: Using AI to learn - The University of Sydney
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Sydney says students should use University-endorsed tools like Copilot and log in with UniKey using Okta.

evidence.sourceTitle: TEQSA Request for Information - University of Sydney Action Plan
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Sydney says it worked with Microsoft to make Copilot for Web available across the institution for free.

records.description: Image-generation AI tool available to staff and some students.
records.howToObtain: Use firefly.adobe.com where faculty/staff entitlement applies.
records.costToUser: Free to all staff; student cost not stated

evidence.sourceTitle: Frequently asked questions about generative AI at Sydney
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Sydney says staff and students in some faculties have access to Adobe Firefly.

evidence.evidenceSnippet: Sydney says Adobe Firefly is available free to all staff at the University.

records.description: Official Cogniti agent for a raw GPT-4 experience.

records.description: AI-detection capability used when misuse of AI in assessment is suspected.
records.howToObtain: Used by markers or teachers when investigating suspected misuse.

evidence.evidenceSnippet: Sydney says Turnitin has a tool for detecting AI use and that it may be used when a marker suspects unpermitted or unacknowledged AI use.
